 {"id":"bd-2b0s","title":"demo_showcase: narrative + scene storyboard","description":"Choose the demo storyline, name, copy, and the ordered scene list (full run + single-scene names).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:12.806761210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:10.217861682Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:10.217788947Z","close_reason":"Completed (spec in README demo_showcase section)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2b0s","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:12.821701513Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":16,"issue_id":"bd-2b0s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Output deliverable

Produce a concrete storyboard so implementation can follow without ambiguity.

### Recommended scene list (example)

1. `hero` – branding + capabilities + palette preview
2. `dashboard` – live layout with services/pipeline/logs
3. `deep_dive_markdown` – release notes / runbook
4. `deep_dive_syntax` – config/code snippet
5. `deep_dive_json` – API payloads
6. `debug_tools` – pretty/inspect + traceback + logging/tracing
7. `export` – HTML/SVG bundle
8. `outro` – summary table/tree + next steps

### Naming

Pick a fictional product name and keep it consistent in copy, e.g. “Nebula Deploy”.

## Acceptance criteria

- Each scene has a 1–2 sentence purpose description.
- Each scene lists which rich_rust features it must exercise.
- Single-scene mode (`--scene <name>`) uses these names verbatim.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2cjt","title":"demo_showcase: interactive start wizard","description":"Add an optional interactive start wizard using Prompt (scenario/speed/export choices).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:20.004600090Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:49.118079718Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","prompt"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","depends_on_id":"bd-12nv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:46.637019230Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:49.118050083Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","depends_on_id":"bd-1o8x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:44.192163526Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","depends_on_id":"bd-3rhg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:20.017001830Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":56,"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Wizard questions (suggested)

- Run mode: full demo vs specific scene
- Speed: normal vs quick
- Export: yes/no

Keep it short and skippable (defaults).

## Acceptance criteria

- Only runs when interactive and not disabled.
- Falls back to defaults otherwise.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2cyz","title":"demo_showcase snapshots: hero scene","description":"Add insta snapshot coverage for the hero/capabilities scene (deterministic output).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:21.282174155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:59.965074132Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","snapshots","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cyz","depends_on_id":"bd-11zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:55.072244250Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cyz","depends_on_id":"bd-3s5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:57.482699738Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cyz","depends_on_id":"bd-5bjf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:59.965042092Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cyz","depends_on_id":"bd-yzr8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:21.298095219Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":84,"issue_id":"bd-2cyz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Snapshot contract

Run `--scene hero` with deterministic flags (seed + fixed dimensions) and snapshot ANSI-stripped output.

Assertions:
- scene header present
- capability table/panel content present
- palette preview present
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:40Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2fl5","title":"demo_showcase: visual design system (theme/palette)","description":"Define a cohesive theme/palette and named styles that make the demo look like a real product.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:17.976813041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:24.788393345Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fl5","depends_on_id":"bd-2b0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:24.788358650Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2fl5","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:18.006687094Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"bd-2fl5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

Make the demo visually consistent and “brandable” using the Theme system and rich_rust style primitives. This is where we turn the output from “works” into “wow”.

## Design principles

- Use a small, consistent palette: primary/accent/success/warn/error + neutrals.
- Prefer named styles via `Theme` so users can copy patterns (`status.ok`, `status.warn`, `panel.title`, etc.).
- Keep readability on low-color terminals: ensure acceptable downgrades to 16/256 colors.
- Use whitespace, rules, and alignment to create hierarchy (like a UI, not a log dump).

## Deliverables

- A `Theme::from_style_definitions(...)` set used by the demo’s Console.
- Named styles for:
  - headers / hero
  - section rules
  - table header + key columns
  - status badges (OK/WARN/ERR)
  - log levels
  - “call to action” links
- Optional: a small helper to render “badges” like `[bold white on green] OK [/ ]`.

## Acceptance criteria

- The demo uses named styles in *most* scenes (not just ad-hoc `Style::parse`).
- The demo still looks coherent when forced to `ColorSystem::Standard`.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:29Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2fl5","title":"demo_showcase: visual design system (theme/palette)","description":"Define a cohesive theme/palette and named styles that make the demo look like a real product.","notes":"Completed via child beads: bd-1by8 (theme/palette definitions in theme.rs) and bd-313x (typography patterns in typography.rs). Named styles include brand.*, status.*, section.*, panel.*, table.*, log.* prefixes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:17.976813041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:43:16.629814050Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:43:16.629672817Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fl5","depends_on_id":"bd-2b0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:24.788358650Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2fl5","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:18.006687094Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"bd-2fl5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

Make the demo visually consistent and “brandable” using the Theme system and rich_rust style primitives. This is where we turn the output from “works” into “wow”.

## Design principles

- Use a small, consistent palette: primary/accent/success/warn/error + neutrals.
- Prefer named styles via `Theme` so users can copy patterns (`status.ok`, `status.warn`, `panel.title`, etc.).
- Keep readability on low-color terminals: ensure acceptable downgrades to 16/256 colors.
- Use whitespace, rules, and alignment to create hierarchy (like a UI, not a log dump).

## Deliverables

- A `Theme::from_style_definitions(...)` set used by the demo’s Console.
- Named styles for:
  - headers / hero
  - section rules
  - table header + key columns
  - status badges (OK/WARN/ERR)
  - log levels
  - “call to action” links
- Optional: a small helper to render “badges” like `[bold white on green] OK [/ ]`.

## Acceptance criteria

- The demo uses named styles in *most* scenes (not just ad-hoc `Style::parse`).
- The demo still looks coherent when forced to `ColorSystem::Standard`.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2hba","title":"demo_showcase: UI helper primitives","description":"Implement small UI helpers (badges, section headers, key/value rows) using Text/Style.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:01.342641761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:25.537563747Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hba","depends_on_id":"bd-1by8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:23.099136270Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hba","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:01.357952838Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hba","depends_on_id":"bd-313x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:25.537534583Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":26,"issue_id":"bd-2hba","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Helpers to build

Small, reusable building blocks for the demo binary:
- `badge(text, style_name)` -> `Text` (or markup snippet)
- `kv_row(key, value)` -> renderable suitable for panels
- `section_title("...")` -> rule + aligned title

These keep the demo consistent and prevent copy/paste styling drift.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2j06","title":"demo_showcase: SVG export writer","description":"Implement SVG export writing and print the output path.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:58.528362072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:46.373008611Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","export","svg"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2j06","depends_on_id":"bd-164e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:58.544199353Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2j06","depends_on_id":"bd-86gp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:46.372982102Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":52,"issue_id":"bd-2j06","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## SVG export

Use `Console::export_svg(true)` (or configured variant) and write to `demo_showcase.svg`.

## Acceptance criteria

- SVG contains representative multi-style output (not just text).
- Demo prints a short note that SVG uses `<foreignObject>` and is best viewed in modern browsers.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:45:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2jec","title":"Conformance: enable fixture-based parity for Syntax/Markdown/JSON","description":"Strengthen Python Rich parity by expanding conformance fixtures beyond plain-text comparisons.

Scope:
- Update Python fixture generator + schema as needed so syntax/markdown/json have stable expectations.
- Normalize unstable fields (theme names, random ids, whitespace) where needed.

Acceptance:
- At least one new fixture each for Syntax/Markdown/JSON where compare_ansi=true (or clearly documented why not).
- Rust conformance test passes under: cargo test --test conformance_python --features "conformance_test,syntax,markdown,json".","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:35:16.789265580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:10.121693324Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:10.121625438Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2jec","depends_on_id":"bd-3c90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:35:16.808226745Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -170,7 +170,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-5bjf","title":"demo_showcase: hero + capabilities scene","description":"Implement the hero/capabilities scene: branding, palette preview, environment detection, hyperlinks, emoji.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:42:06.963744820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:53.120813985Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","hero"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5bjf","depends_on_id":"bd-1by8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:50.752840213Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-5bjf","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:48.352025280Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-5bjf","depends_on_id":"bd-2175","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:42:06.976533058Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-5bjf","depends_on_id":"bd-313x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:53.120787285Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":36,"issue_id":"bd-5bjf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Content requirements

- Big branded title (aligned/centered) + short tagline.
- Capability panel showing:
  - width/height
  - detected color system
  - is_terminal / is_interactive
  - emoji enabled
- Palette preview: small table or columns block showing primary/accent/status colors.
- A hyperlink CTA (docs + repo).

## Acceptance criteria

- This scene alone should convince a user the library is polished.
- Works in non-TTY mode (prints static content; no prompts).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:42:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-61ky","title":"demo_showcase tests: feature gating behavior","description":"Add tests for feature-gated scenes: when features are off, output includes enable commands; when on, output renders real content.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:17.320950396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:27.086596657Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","features","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-61ky","depends_on_id":"bd-11zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:13.137472850Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61ky","depends_on_id":"bd-21ef","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:22.393590221Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61ky","depends_on_id":"bd-26gp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:17.798870508Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61ky","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:17.336257448Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61ky","depends_on_id":"bd-325x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:27.086563946Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61ky","depends_on_id":"bd-3jff","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:24.739709353Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61ky","depends_on_id":"bd-3pwg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:20.104130064Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61ky","depends_on_id":"bd-3w3u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:15.459762901Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":72,"issue_id":"bd-61ky","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## What to test

For each feature-gated scene:
- When the feature is **disabled**, the scene must still run and print a styled panel containing:
  - feature name
  - exact enable command (`--features ...`)

When the feature is **enabled**, it must render real content (not just the enable panel).

## Acceptance criteria

- Gating behavior is consistent across Markdown/Syntax/JSON/Tracing scenes.
- Enable commands are correct and kept in sync with the chosen feature strategy (bd-26gp).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:11:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6ozb","title":"demo_showcase: final polish + QA","description":"Polish pass: visual tweaks, timing, edge cases; ensure all quality gates pass and demo feels ‘product grade’.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:28.642592479Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:17.783744643Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","polish","qa"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-164e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:15.925273437Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-2175","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:08.824416694Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:35.835459296Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-2smc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:06.360421270Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-2xsi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:13.617768058Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-3bid","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:18.150875345Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:28.660699129Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-3rhg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:22.625349203Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-3vf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:11.348492415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-ohsq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:20.376258928Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":14,"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Checklist

- Run full quality gates:
  - `cargo fmt --check`
  - `cargo check --all-targets`
  - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  - `cargo test`
- Visual pass on at least:
  - wide terminal (>= 120 cols)
  - narrow terminal (~70 cols)
  - piped output (`| cat`)
- Ensure the demo ends cleanly: cursor restored, alt-screen disabled, no stray control codes.

## Acceptance criteria

- Demo looks “designed”, not like a pile of examples.
- All scenes have consistent titles, spacing, and style usage.
- Total runtime feels appropriate; `--quick` exists for CI/dev.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:37Z"},{"id":77,"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Update: automated regression suite is required

Final QA must include the automated demo_showcase regression suite (`bd-2sk1`):
- per-scene smoke tests
- snapshot tests
- export bundle e2e tests
- non-interactive safety tests

This reduces reliance on manual eyeballing and prevents “works on my terminal” regressions.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:17Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-6tj5","title":"demo_showcase tests: CLI + Config unit tests","description":"Expand unit tests for CLI/config: defaults, invalid flags, and new flags like --seed/--links.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:04.773473520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:16.075367819Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6tj5","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:04.794750840Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6tj5","depends_on_id":"bd-3s5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:16.075340749Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":67,"issue_id":"bd-6tj5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit test scope

Expand unit tests inside `src/bin/demo_showcase.rs` to cover:
- default config values (including derived defaults like interactive/live/screen auto modes)
- parsing of numeric flags (`--speed`, `--width`, `--height`, `--seed`)
- boolean flag pairs (`--foo` / `--no-foo`)
- mutual exclusion rules (e.g. `--export` vs `--export-dir`)
- friendly error messages

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests are deterministic and do not require a TTY.
- Test failures point directly to the CLI regression.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:52Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-6tj5","title":"demo_showcase tests: CLI + Config unit tests","description":"Expand unit tests for CLI/config: defaults, invalid flags, and new flags like --seed/--links.","notes":"Added 12 new unit tests covering: default config values, --quick, --force-terminal, -h short form, all color system variants, missing flag values, non-finite/negative speed, all boolean pairs, width/height non-integer, positional arg rejection, and combined flags.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:04.773473520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:46:48.320559684Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:46:48.320428780Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6tj5","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:04.794750840Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6tj5","depends_on_id":"bd-3s5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:16.075340749Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":67,"issue_id":"bd-6tj5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit test scope

Expand unit tests inside `src/bin/demo_showcase.rs` to cover:
- default config values (including derived defaults like interactive/live/screen auto modes)
- parsing of numeric flags (`--speed`, `--width`, `--height`, `--seed`)
- boolean flag pairs (`--foo` / `--no-foo`)
- mutual exclusion rules (e.g. `--export` vs `--export-dir`)
- friendly error messages

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests are deterministic and do not require a TTY.
- Test failures point directly to the CLI regression.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-86gp","title":"demo_showcase: capture output for export","description":"Add capture mode to demo runner so scenes can be captured to segments for exporting.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:53.628166487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:41.570694049Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","export"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-86gp","depends_on_id":"bd-12nv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:41.570665666Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-86gp","depends_on_id":"bd-164e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:53.642928274Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-86gp","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:39.207097640Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":50,"issue_id":"bd-86gp","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Approach

- When `--export` or `--export-dir` is set, run the demo with capture enabled:
  - `console.begin_capture()`
  - run selected scenes
  - then call `export_html` / `export_svg`

Be careful: capture + Live/alt-screen don't mix well. For export runs, prefer non-live mode (static output).

## Acceptance criteria

- Export run produces stable artifacts without requiring a TTY.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:45:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-abyl","title":"demo_showcase: dashboard layout fallback (narrow)","description":"Implement a narrow terminal fallback layout (collapse sidebar / stack panels).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:52.679493487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:10.518019854Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-abyl","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:10.517989587Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-abyl","depends_on_id":"bd-2smc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:52.704817099Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":31,"issue_id":"bd-abyl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Narrow-mode behavior

When width is below a threshold (e.g. < 80):
- collapse sidebar beneath main
- reduce table columns / abbreviate labels
- prefer single-column stacking over cramped two-column layout

## Acceptance criteria

- No panics due to zero-width regions.
- Narrow mode still looks intentional and readable.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:41:25Z"}]}
